\chapter{Boolean Analysis — Gate Switching}
%%% generated by the blueprint pipeline from TCSlib.BooleanAnalysis.LMN.GateSwitching
\section{Overview}

This module is Step~4 of the LMN argument: it applies Håstad's switching lemma to
individual gates after a Bernoulli$(p)$ restriction. The key bridge results show that,
with high probability, the restriction of a bounded-width $\mathrm{DNF}$ (or $\mathrm{CNF}$)
gate has small decision-tree depth and hence admits a small-width $\mathrm{CNF}$ (or
$\mathrm{DNF}$) representation, and assemble these into union bounds over all gates of a
circuit layer.

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
\section{Declarations}

\begin{lemma}[Monotonicity of the Bernoulli restriction probability]
\lean{LMN.bernoulliRestrProb_mono}
\leanok
\uses{SwitchingLemma2.Restriction, SwitchingLemma2.bernoulliRestrProb, SwitchingLemma2.bernoulliRestrWeight_nonneg'}
Let $p\in[0,1]$ and let $A,B$ be predicates on restrictions. If $A(\rho)$ implies $B(\rho)$
for every restriction $\rho$, then
\[
  \mathrm{bernoulliRestrProb}\,p\,A \le \mathrm{bernoulliRestrProb}\,p\,B .
\]
\end{lemma}

\begin{theorem}[Restricted DNF gate has a small CNF, with high probability]
\lean{LMN.switching_bernoulli_gate_to_cnf}
\leanok
\uses{CNF, CNF.eval, CNF.width, DNF, DNF.eval, DNF.width, LMN.bernoulliRestrProb_mono, SwitchingBernoulli.switching_bernoulli_dtDepth_dnf, SwitchingLemma2.bernoulliRestrProb, SwitchingLemma2.dtDepth_le_implies_small_dnf_cnf, SwitchingLemma2.restrictFn}
Let $g$ be a $\mathrm{DNF}$ of width at most $w>0$ (with distinct variables per term and
no duplicate literals), let $n>0$, and let $0<p\le \tfrac{1}{40w}$ with $p\le 1$. Then the
probability under a Bernoulli$(p)$ restriction $\rho$ that $g|_\rho$ cannot be expressed as a
$\mathrm{CNF}$ of width at most $l$ is at most
\[
  \Bigl(\tfrac12\Bigr)^l + \exp\!\Bigl(-\tfrac{np}{3}\Bigr).
\]
\end{theorem}

\begin{theorem}[Restricted CNF gate has a small DNF, with high probability]
\lean{LMN.switching_bernoulli_gate_to_dnf_from_cnf}
\leanok
\uses{CNF, CNF.eval, CNF.width, DNF, DNF.eval, DNF.width, LMN.bernoulliRestrProb_mono, SwitchingBernoulli.switching_bernoulli_dtDepth_cnf, SwitchingLemma2.bernoulliRestrProb, SwitchingLemma2.dtDepth_le_implies_small_dnf_cnf, SwitchingLemma2.restrictFn}
The dual statement. Let $g$ be a $\mathrm{CNF}$ of width at most $w>0$ (with distinct
variables per clause and no duplicate literals), let $n>0$, and let $0<p\le \tfrac{1}{40w}$
with $p\le 1$. Then the probability under a Bernoulli$(p)$ restriction $\rho$ that $g|_\rho$
cannot be expressed as a $\mathrm{DNF}$ of width at most $l$ is at most
\[
  \Bigl(\tfrac12\Bigr)^l + \exp\!\Bigl(-\tfrac{np}{3}\Bigr).
\]
\end{theorem}

\begin{theorem}[Small decision-tree depth yields a small CNF]
\lean{LMN.restricted_has_small_cnf_of_dtDepth_le}
\leanok
\uses{CNF, CNF.eval, CNF.width, SwitchingLemma2.Restriction, SwitchingLemma2.dtDepth_le_implies_small_dnf_cnf, SwitchingLemma2.restrictFn, dtDepth}
If the restricted function $f|_\rho$ has decision-tree depth at most $l$, then there exists a
$\mathrm{CNF}$ $\psi$ of width at most $l$ that computes $f|_\rho$ on every input.
\end{theorem}

\begin{theorem}[Small decision-tree depth yields a small DNF]
\lean{LMN.restricted_has_small_dnf_of_dtDepth_le}
\leanok
\uses{DNF, DNF.eval, DNF.width, SwitchingLemma2.Restriction, SwitchingLemma2.dtDepth_le_implies_small_dnf_cnf, SwitchingLemma2.restrictFn, dtDepth}
If the restricted function $f|_\rho$ has decision-tree depth at most $l$, then there exists a
$\mathrm{DNF}$ $\varphi$ of width at most $l$ that computes $f|_\rho$ on every input.
\end{theorem}

\begin{theorem}[Union bound on gates with large decision-tree depth]
\lean{LMN.switching_bernoulli_union_bound}
\leanok
\uses{DNF, DNF.eval, DNF.width, SwitchingBernoulli.switching_bernoulli_dtDepth_dnf, SwitchingLemma2.Restriction, SwitchingLemma2.bernoulliRestrProb, SwitchingLemma2.restrictFn, dtDepth}
Let $\mathrm{gates}_0,\dots,\mathrm{gates}_{s-1}$ be $s$ many $\mathrm{DNF}$s, each of width at
most $w>0$ (with distinct variables per term and no duplicate literals), let $n>0$, and let
$0<p\le \tfrac{1}{40w}$ with $p\le 1$. Then the probability under a Bernoulli$(p)$ restriction
$\rho$ that some gate $\mathrm{gates}_i$ has $\mathrm{dtDepth}(\mathrm{gates}_i|_\rho) > l$ is
at most
\[
  s\cdot\Bigl(\Bigl(\tfrac12\Bigr)^l + \exp\!\Bigl(-\tfrac{np}{3}\Bigr)\Bigr).
\]
\end{theorem}

\begin{theorem}[All gates admit a small CNF under a good restriction]
\lean{LMN.all_gates_have_small_cnf}
\leanok
\uses{CNF, CNF.eval, CNF.width, DNF, DNF.eval, LMN.restricted_has_small_cnf_of_dtDepth_le, SwitchingLemma2.Restriction, SwitchingLemma2.restrictFn, dtDepth}
Given a family of $\mathrm{DNF}$ gates and a restriction $\rho$ under which every gate
satisfies $\mathrm{dtDepth}(\mathrm{gates}_i|_\rho)\le l$, every gate can be replaced by a
$\mathrm{CNF}$ of width at most $l$ computing $\mathrm{gates}_i|_\rho$ on all inputs.
\end{theorem}

\begin{theorem}[Union bound for layer-2 CNF replaceability]
\lean{LMN.layer2_cnf_replaceability_union_bound}
\leanok
\uses{CNF, CNF.eval, CNF.width, DNF, DNF.eval, DNF.width, LMN.bernoulliRestrProb_mono, LMN.restricted_has_small_cnf_of_dtDepth_le, LMN.switching_bernoulli_union_bound, SwitchingLemma2.bernoulliRestrProb, SwitchingLemma2.restrictFn}
Let $\mathrm{gates}_0,\dots,\mathrm{gates}_{s_2-1}$ be the layer-2 $\mathrm{DNF}$ gates, each of
width at most $w>0$ (with distinct variables per term and no duplicate literals), let $n>0$,
and let $0<p\le \tfrac{1}{40w}$ with $p\le 1$. Then the probability under a Bernoulli$(p)$
restriction $\rho$ that some gate fails to admit a width-$l$ $\mathrm{CNF}$ representation of
$\mathrm{gates}_i|_\rho$ is at most
\[
  s_2\cdot\Bigl(\Bigl(\tfrac12\Bigr)^l + \exp\!\Bigl(-\tfrac{np}{3}\Bigr)\Bigr).
\]
\end{theorem}

\begin{theorem}[Simplified layer-2 CNF replaceability bound]
\lean{LMN.layer2_cnf_replaceability_simplified}
\leanok
\uses{CNF, CNF.eval, CNF.width, DNF, DNF.eval, DNF.width, LMN.layer2_cnf_replaceability_union_bound, SwitchingLemma2.bernoulliRestrProb, SwitchingLemma2.restrictFn}
Under the same hypotheses as the previous theorem, fix $\varepsilon>0$ and $s_2>0$, and suppose
$n$ is large enough that $\exp\!\bigl(-\tfrac{np}{3}\bigr)\le \tfrac{\varepsilon}{s_2}$. Then the
probability under a Bernoulli$(p)$ restriction $\rho$ that some layer-2 gate fails to admit a
width-$l$ $\mathrm{CNF}$ representation of $\mathrm{gates}_i|_\rho$ is at most
\[
  s_2\cdot\Bigl(\tfrac12\Bigr)^l + \varepsilon .
\]
\end{theorem}
